title: (No AI) YouTube clarifies policies around AI slop and upsetting videos

content: YouTube has updated its monetization policies to more clearly define the kinds of AI-generated and low-quality videos that can’t earn ad revenue.

title: (No AI) Adobe’s ‘natural look’ camera app embraces generative AI
author: Jess Weatherbed
content:
![Two before and after images showing photographs edited with generative AI in Adobe’s Indigo app.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/Adobe-Indigo-AI-Playground-hero.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Welcome to the “what is a photo” debate, Adobe. | Image: Adobe

Adobe's experimental camera app has taken an unexpected turn. After Project Indigo was launched last year to provide a "more natural (SLR-like) look" for iPhone photography, the Indigo camera app is now being updated with a suite of generative AI tools. And the change doesn't rely upon Adobe's own Firefly AI models.

Adobe describes the new "AI Playground" tooling suite as an experiment and says there's a button that allows users to opt out and continue using the app as before. Free access to the suite with no sign-on requirement is being tested with a small percentage of Indigo users over the next few weeks. "Our goal is to learn how people …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/967791/adobe-indigo-camera-app-ai-playground-update)

Welcome to the “what is a photo” debate, Adobe. | Image: Adobe	

Adobe's experimental camera app has taken an unexpected turn. And the change doesn't rely upon Adobe's own Firefly AI models.
Adobe describes the new "AI Playground" tooling suite as an experiment and says there's a button that allows users to opt out and continue using the app as before. Free access to the suite with no sign-on requirement is being tested with a small percentage of Indigo users over the next few weeks. "Our goal is to learn how people …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) China delivers a one-two punch to America’s AI dominance
author: Robert Hart
content:
![SHANGHAI, CHINA - JULY 20 2026: People visit the booth of Kimi, an LLM developed by the Chinese startup Moonshot, during the World AI Conference in Shanghai, China, Monday, July 20, 2026. (Photo credit should read LONG WEI/ Feature China/Future Publishing via Getty Images)](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/gettyimages-2286280034.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

China's leading AI companies are ramping up the pressure on Silicon Valley, as Moonshot and Alibaba unveiled models they claim can go toe-to-toe with the best from OpenAI and Anthropic at a fraction of the cost. The rapid-fire releases suggest America's lead at the AI frontier is increasingly tight, just as the technology is becoming central to national security, economic power, and geopolitical influence.

The opening salvo came from Beijing-based Moonshot AI, one of China's leading AI model developers, which [unveiled Kimi K3](https://x.com/Kimi_Moonshot/status/2077830229968683203?s=20) on Friday. Moonshot [claims](https://www.kimi.com/blog/kimi-k3) its own testing ranks it consistently above nearly every US system, trailing only OpenAI …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/967781/chinese-ai-models-open-source-moonshot-kimi-k3-alibaba-qwen)

title: (No AI) How Google’s New Gemini Rates Work and How to Track Your Usage
